metadata.summary: Tornado websocket backend for the Xterm.js Javascript terminal emulator library.

documentation:
This is a [Tornado](http://tornadoweb.org/) websocket backend for the
[Xterm.js](https://xtermjs.org/) Javascript terminal emulator
library.

It evolved out of [pyxterm](https://github.com/mitotic/pyxterm), which was
part of [GraphTerm](https://github.com/mitotic/graphterm) (as lineterm.py),
v0.57.0 (2014-07-18), and ultimately derived from the public-domain [Ajaxterm](http://antony.lesuisse.org/software/ajaxterm/) code, v0.11 (2008-11-13) (also
on Github as part of [QWeb](https://github.com/antonylesuisse/qweb)).

Modules:

- `terminado.management`: controls launching virtual terminals,
  connecting them to Tornado's event loop, and closing them down.
- `terminado.websocket`: Provides a websocket handler for communicating with
  a terminal.
- `terminado.uimodule`: Provides a `Terminal` Tornado [UI Module](http://www.tornadoweb.org/en/stable/guide/templates.html#ui-modules).

JS:

- `terminado/_static/terminado.js`: A lightweight wrapper to set up a
  term.js terminal with a websocket.

Local Installation:

> \$ pip install -e .\[test\]

Usage example:

```python
import os.path
import tornado.web
import tornado.ioloop
# This demo requires tornado_xstatic and XStatic-term.js
import tornado_xstatic

import terminado
STATIC_DIR = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(terminado.__file__), "_static")

class TerminalPageHandler(tornado.web.RequestHandler):
    def get(self):
        return self.render("termpage.html", static=self.static_url,
                           xstatic=self.application.settings['xstatic_url'],
                           ws_url_path="/websocket")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    term_manager = terminado.SingleTermManager(shell_command=['bash'])
    handlers = [
                (r"/websocket", terminado.TermSocket,
                     {'term_manager': term_manager}),
                (r"/", TerminalPageHandler),
                (r"/xstatic/(.*)", tornado_xstatic.XStaticFileHandler,
                     {'allowed_modules': ['termjs']})
               ]
    app = tornado.web.Application(handlers, static_path=STATIC_DIR,
                      xstatic_url = tornado_xstatic.url_maker('/xstatic/'))
    # Serve at http://localhost:8765/ N.B. Leaving out 'localhost' here will
    # work, but it will listen on the public network interface as well.
    # Given what terminado does, that would be rather a security hole.
    app.listen(8765, 'localhost')
    try:
        tornado.ioloop.IOLoop.instance().start()
    finally:
        term_manager.shutdown()
```

See the [demos directory](https://github.com/takluyver/terminado/tree/master/demos)
for more examples. This is a simplified version of the `single.py` demo.

Run the unit tests with:

> \$ pytest

modules.name: terminado
documentation: Terminals served to xterm.js using Tornado websockets

modules.name: terminado.uimodule
documentation:
A Tornado UI module for a terminal backed by terminado.

See the Tornado docs for information on UI modules:
http://www.tornadoweb.org/en/stable/guide/templates.html#ui-modules

modules.name: terminado.websocket
documentation: Tornado websocket handler to serve a terminal interface.

classes.name: terminado.websocket.TermSocket

methods.name: terminado.websocket.TermSocket.origin_check
documentation: Deprecated: backward-compat for terminado <= 0.5.

methods.name: terminado.websocket.TermSocket.open
documentation:
Websocket connection opened.

Call our terminal manager to get a terminal, and connect to it as a
client.

methods.name: terminado.websocket.TermSocket.on_pty_read
documentation: Data read from pty; send to frontend

methods.name: terminado.websocket.TermSocket.on_message
documentation:
Handle incoming websocket message

We send JSON arrays, where the first element is a string indicating
what kind of message this is. Data associated with the message follows.

methods.name: terminado.websocket.TermSocket.on_close
documentation:
Handle websocket closing.

Disconnect from our terminal, and tell the terminal manager we're
disconnecting.

methods.name: terminado.websocket.TermSocket.on_pty_died
documentation: Terminal closed: tell the frontend, and close the socket.

methods.name: terminado.websocket.TermSocket.log_terminal_output
documentation:
Logs the terminal input/output
:param log: log line to write
:return:

methods.name: terminado.websocket.TermSocket.stdin_to_ptyproc
documentation:
Handles stdin messages sent on the websocket.

This is a blocking call that should NOT be performed inside the
server primary event loop thread. Messages must be handled
asynchronously to prevent blocking on the PTY buffer.
Handler for a terminal websocket

classes.name: terminado.tests.basic_test.TestTermClient

methods.name: terminado.tests.basic_test.TestTermClient.read_all_msg
documentation: Read messages until read times out

methods.name: terminado.tests.basic_test.TestTermClient.read_stdout
documentation:
Read standard output until timeout read reached,
return stdout and any non-stdout msgs received.

methods.name: terminado.tests.basic_test.TestTermClient.discard_stdout
documentation:
Read standard output messages, discarding the data
as it's received. Return the number of bytes discarded
and any non-stdout msgs

methods.name: terminado.tests.basic_test.TestTermClient.write_stdin
documentation: Write to terminal stdin

methods.name: terminado.tests.basic_test.TestTermClient.get_pid
documentation: Get process ID of terminal shell process
Test connection to a terminal manager

methods.name: terminado.tests.basic_test.TermTestCase.get_term_client
documentation: Factory for TestTermClient, because it has to be async
See:  https://github.com/tornadoweb/tornado/issues/1161

modules.name: terminado.management
documentation: Terminal management for exposing terminals to a web interface using Tornado.

functions.name: terminado.management._update_removing
documentation: Like dict.update(), but remove keys where the value is None.

functions.name: terminado.management._poll
documentation: Poll using poll() on posix systems and select() elsewhere (e.g., Windows)

methods.name: terminado.management.PtyWithClients.resize_to_smallest
documentation:
Set the terminal size to that of the smallest client dimensions.

A terminal not using the full space available is much nicer than a
terminal trying to use more than the available space, so we keep it
sized to the smallest client.

methods.name: terminado.management.PtyWithClients.kill
documentation: Send a signal to the process in the pty

methods.name: terminado.management.PtyWithClients.killpg
documentation: Send a signal to the process group of the process in the pty

methods.name: terminado.management.PtyWithClients.terminate
documentation:
This forces a child process to terminate. It starts nicely with
SIGHUP and SIGINT. If "force" is True then moves onto SIGKILL. This
returns True if the child was terminated. This returns False if the
child could not be terminated.

classes.name: terminado.management.TermManagerBase

methods.name: terminado.management.TermManagerBase.make_term_env
documentation: Build the environment variables for the process in the terminal.

methods.name: terminado.management.TermManagerBase.new_terminal
documentation: Make a new terminal, return a {class}`PtyWithClients` instance.

methods.name: terminado.management.TermManagerBase.start_reading
documentation: Connect a terminal to the tornado event loop to read data from it.

methods.name: terminado.management.TermManagerBase.on_eof
documentation: Called when the pty has closed.

methods.name: terminado.management.TermManagerBase.pty_read
documentation: Called by the event loop when there is pty data ready to read.

methods.name: terminado.management.TermManagerBase.pre_pty_read_hook
documentation: Hook before pty read, subclass can patch something into ptywclients when pty_read

methods.name: terminado.management.TermManagerBase.get_terminal
documentation:
Override in a subclass to give a terminal to a new websocket connection

The {class}`TermSocket` handler works with zero or one URL components
(capturing groups in the URL spec regex). If it receives one, it is
passed as the `url_component` parameter; otherwise, this is None.

methods.name: terminado.management.TermManagerBase.client_disconnected
documentation: Override this to e.g. kill terminals on client disconnection.
Base class for a terminal manager.

classes.name: terminado.management.SingleTermManager
documentation: All connections to the websocket share a common terminal.

classes.name: terminado.management.UniqueTermManager

methods.name: terminado.management.UniqueTermManager.client_disconnected
documentation: Send terminal SIGHUP when client disconnects.
Give each websocket a unique terminal to use.

classes.name: terminado.management.NamedTermManager
documentation: Share terminals between websockets connected to the same endpoint.
